chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-4880 of 2016 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Application in d/o case For hearing of CMA 6315/2021(C/A) 20-08-2025 Hadi Bux Maitlo (Petitioner) is present in person. M/s Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant A.G and Gulzar Ahmed Malano, Assistant P.G a/w Gulzar on behalf of SSP, Khairpur and Inspector Gul Muhammad, SHO, P.S, B-section, Khairpur. Pervez Ahmed Solangi (respondent No.17) is present in person. **** Record reflects that instant petition was disposed of by this Court through order dated 17.11.2017, whereby the police was directed to ensure that no untoward incident occurs within their remit and at the subject lands, situated in Deh Luqman, Taluka and District Khairpur; and that if any party is aggrieved, protection shall be provided to them within the parameters of law. Today, on the listed contempt application filed by the petitioner, learned Law Officers file statements on behalf of SSP Khairpur and SHO, P.S. B-Section, Khairpur (respondents No.2 & 4), which are taken on record, and copies thereof are provided to the petitioner. SHO, PS, B- section, Khairpur submits that he shall act strictly in accordance with law and that no harassment shall be caused to the petitioner. Private respondent No.17 is present and submits that the petitioner has already filed Civil Revision No.S-120 of 2021 against the private respondents, which is pending before this Court. After such statements, the petitioner, who maintains the listed application in person, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the same. Accordingly, the listed application is disposed of with directions to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to either party.

After such statements, the petitioner, who maintains the listed application in person, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the same. Accordingly, the listed application is disposed of with directions to the official respondents to conduct themselves strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to either party. However, it is made clear that this order shall not come in the way of any 2 criminal, civil, or family proceedings, if pending between the parties before the competent Courts of law. JUDGE Ahmad
